8	// PackageType represents the type of a registry package.
9	type PackageType string
10	
11	const (
12		PackageTypeWorkflow     PackageType = "workflow"
13		PackageTypePlugin       PackageType = "plugin"
14		PackageTypePersonaPack  PackageType = "persona-pack"
15		PackageTypeTemplatePack PackageType = "template-pack"
16		PackageTypeResource     PackageType = "resource"
17	)
18	
19	// InstallMapping describes a source→target copy during installation.
20	type InstallMapping struct {
21		Source string `yaml:"source"`
22		Target string `yaml:"target"`
23	}
24	
25	// PackageInstall describes what to copy during installation.
26	type PackageInstall struct {
27		Root       *InstallMapping  `yaml:"root,omitempty"`       // plugin root (e.g., .ddx/plugins/helix)
28		Skills     []InstallMapping `yaml:"skills,omitempty"`     // skills directories (copied to each target)
29		Scripts    *InstallMapping  `yaml:"scripts,omitempty"`    // scripts/binaries
30		Symlinks   []SymlinkMapping `yaml:"symlinks,omitempty"`   // post-install symlinks
31		Executable []string         `yaml:"executable,omitempty"` // paths (relative to root) that must be executable
32	}
33	
34	// SymlinkMapping describes a symlink to create during installation.
35	type SymlinkMapping struct {
36		Source string `yaml:"source"` // the source path (relative to install root)
37		Target string `yaml:"target"` // the target path (where symlink points)
38	}
39	
40	// Package describes a single installable package.
41	type Package struct {
42		Name        string         `yaml:"name"`
43		Version     string         `yaml:"version"`
44		Description string         `yaml:"description"`
45		Type        PackageType    `yaml:"type"`
46		Source      string         `yaml:"source"`
47		APIVersion  string         `yaml:"api_version,omitempty"`
48		Install     PackageInstall `yaml:"install"`
49		Keywords    []string       `yaml:"keywords,omitempty"`
50		// Extra captures top-level keys not recognized by this DDx version so
51		// newer manifests round-trip without silently dropping fields. See
52		// SD-018 "Manifest Versioning". Not marshaled via `yaml:"-"`; use
53		// MarshalPackage for round-trip serialization.
54		Extra map[string]any `yaml:"-"`
55	}
56	
57	// Registry holds the list of known packages.
58	type Registry struct {
59		Packages []Package
60	}
61	
62	// BuiltinRegistry returns the built-in registry of known packages.
63	func BuiltinRegistry() *Registry {
64		return &Registry{
65			Packages: []Package{
66				{
67					Name:        "ddx",
68					Version:     "0.4.7",
69					Description: "DDx default library: prompts, personas, MCP configs, environments, skills",
70					Type:        PackageTypePlugin,
71					Source:      "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx",
72					Install: PackageInstall{
73						Root: &InstallMapping{
74							Source: "library",
75							Target: ".ddx/plugins/ddx",
76						},
77						Skills: []InstallMapping{
78							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: ".agents/skills/"},
79							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: ".claude/skills/"},
80							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: "~/.agents/skills/"},
81							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: "~/.claude/skills/"},
82						},
83					},
84					Keywords: []string{"library", "prompts", "personas", "mcp", "default", "skills"},
85				},
86				{
87					Name:        "helix",
88					Version:     "0.3.2",
89					Description: "Supervisory autopilot for AI-assisted software delivery",
90					Type:        PackageTypeWorkflow,
91					Source:      "https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/helix",
92					Install: PackageInstall{
93						// Copy plugin to global ~/.ddx/plugins/ so it persists
94						// across projects and global symlinks (scripts, skills)
95						// resolve correctly.
96						Root: &InstallMapping{
97							Source: ".",
98							Target: "~/.ddx/plugins/helix",
99						},
100						// Skills installed to project-local and global skill directories.
101						// Project-local (.agents/skills/, .claude/skills/) enables
102						// per-project skill resolution. Global (~/.agents/skills/,
103						// ~/.claude/skills/) enables skills outside of any project.
104						Skills: []InstallMapping{
105							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: ".agents/skills/"},
106							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: ".claude/skills/"},
107							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: "~/.agents/skills/"},
108							{Source: ".agents/skills/", Target: "~/.claude/skills/"},
109						},
110						// CLI script → ~/.local/bin/helix
111						// Uses scripts/helix directly (bin/helix has a symlink
112						// resolution bug when invoked through a symlink).
113						Scripts: &InstallMapping{
114							Source: "scripts/helix",
115							Target: "~/.local/bin/helix",
116						},
117						Executable: []string{
118							"scripts/helix",
119						},
120					},
121					Keywords: []string{"workflow", "methodology", "ai", "development"},
122				},
123			},
124		}
125	}

127	// Find returns the package with the given name, or an error if not found.
128	func (r *Registry) Find(name string) (*Package, error) {
129		for i := range r.Packages {
130			if r.Packages[i].Name == name {
131				return &r.Packages[i], nil
132			}
133		}
134		return nil, fmt.Errorf("package %q not found in registry", name)
135	}
136	
137	// Search returns all packages whose name, description, or keywords contain the query.
138	func (r *Registry) Search(query string) []Package {
139		q := strings.ToLower(query)
140		var results []Package
141		for _, pkg := range r.Packages {
142			if matchesQuery(pkg, q) {
143				results = append(results, pkg)
144			}
145		}
146		return results
147	}
148	
149	func matchesQuery(pkg Package, query string) bool {
150		if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(pkg.Name), query) {
151			return true
152		}
153		if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(pkg.Description), query) {
154			return true
155		}
156		if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(string(pkg.Type)), query) {
157			return true
158		}
159		for _, kw := range pkg.Keywords {
160			if strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(kw), query) {
161				return true
162			}
163		}
164		return false
165	}
166	
167	// IsResourcePath returns true if name looks like a resource path (e.g. "persona/foo").
168	func IsResourcePath(name string) bool {
169		return strings.Contains(name, "/")
170	}

240	func TestSymlinkSkills_BrokenTarballSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
241		// Simulate the GitHub tarball bug: .agents/skills/ contains symlinks
242		// with absolute paths from the build machine (e.g. /home/erik/Projects/helix/skills/...)
243		// that don't exist on the installing machine.
244		root := t.TempDir()
245	
246		// Create the real skill directories (as they exist after Root copy)
247		realSkillDir := filepath.Join(root, "skills", "helix-test")
248		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(realSkillDir, 0o755))
249		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(realSkillDir, "SKILL.md"), []byte("test"), 0o644))
250	
251		// Create broken symlinks (as tarball would produce)
252		agentsSkillDir := filepath.Join(root, ".agents", "skills")
253		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentsSkillDir, 0o755))
254		// This symlink points to an absolute path that doesn't exist
255		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink("/nonexistent/build-machine/skills/helix-test",
256			filepath.Join(agentsSkillDir, "helix-test")))
257	
258		// symlinkSkills should recover by finding skills/helix-test in the root
259		dstDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".claude", "skills")
260		written, err := symlinkSkills(root, &InstallMapping{
261			Source: ".agents/skills/",
262			Target: dstDir,
263		})
264		require.NoError(t, err)
265		require.Len(t, written, 1, "should have created 1 symlink despite broken source symlink")
266	
267		// The created symlink should point to the real skill dir
268		target, err := os.Readlink(filepath.Join(dstDir, "helix-test"))
269		require.NoError(t, err)
270		assert.Contains(t, target, "skills/helix-test")
271	
272		// And the target should actually exist
273		_, err = os.Stat(filepath.Join(dstDir, "helix-test", "SKILL.md"))
274		assert.NoError(t, err, "skill content should be accessible through symlink")
275	}

345	// Bug B regression (ddx-6365b2b3): when the plugin root is itself a
346	// symlink pointing outside the project tree, link-target computation must
347	// stay logical (in-repo). EvalSymlinks-resolved paths escape the project
348	// and produce repo-leaving committed links that oscillate per-machine.
349	func TestSymlinkSkills_PluginRootSymlinkDoesNotEscape(t *testing.T) {
350		// Layout:
351		//   <project>/.ddx/plugins/foo  → /tmp/<global>/.ddx/plugins/foo (symlink)
352		//   /tmp/<global>/.ddx/plugins/foo/.agents/skills/sample/SKILL.md (real)
353		//   <project>/.agents/skills/   (link target)
354		//
355		// Expected: <project>/.agents/skills/sample → ../../.ddx/plugins/foo/.agents/skills/sample
356		// Bug: <project>/.agents/skills/sample → ../../../../<absolute>/skills/sample
357		project := t.TempDir()
358		global := t.TempDir()
359	
360		realPluginRoot := filepath.Join(global, ".ddx", "plugins", "foo")
361		skillDir := filepath.Join(realPluginRoot, ".agents", "skills", "sample")
362		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0o755))
363		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md"),
364			[]byte("---\nname: sample\ndescription: Sample\n---\n"), 0o644))
365	
366		// Project-local plugin path is a symlink → global plugin root.
367		projectPluginParent := filepath.Join(project, ".ddx", "plugins")
368		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(projectPluginParent, 0o755))
369		projectPluginRoot := filepath.Join(projectPluginParent, "foo")
370		require.NoError(t, os.Symlink(realPluginRoot, projectPluginRoot))
371	
372		dstDir := filepath.Join(project, ".agents", "skills")
373		written, err := symlinkSkills(projectPluginRoot, &InstallMapping{
374			Source: ".agents/skills/",
375			Target: dstDir,
376		})
377		require.NoError(t, err)
378		require.Len(t, written, 1)
379	
380		linkPath := filepath.Join(dstDir, "sample")
381		target, err := os.Readlink(linkPath)
382		require.NoError(t, err)
383		assert.False(t, filepath.IsAbs(target),
384			"link target should be relative; absolute paths leak the build machine: %q", target)
385		assert.NotContains(t, target, global,
386			"link target should NOT reference the global plugin root path %q (escapes project): %q", global, target)
387		// Resolved logical path stays inside the project.
388		resolved := filepath.Clean(filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(linkPath), target))
389		assert.True(t, strings.HasPrefix(resolved, project),
390			"resolved link target %q should stay within project %q", resolved, project)
391	}

512	// FEAT-015 §5 / AC-004: plugin skill symlinks must be RELATIVE so they
513	// survive clones, home-directory moves, and tarball rebuilds on a
514	// different machine. This test asserts the link target is a relative
515	// path and that it resolves back to the real skill content.
516	func TestSymlinkSkills_WritesRelativeSymlinks(t *testing.T) {
517		projectRoot := t.TempDir()

520		// a skill at .agents/skills/helix-align (real directory).
521		pluginRoot := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "plugins", "helix")
522		realSkillDir := filepath.Join(pluginRoot, ".agents", "skills", "helix-align")

525	
526		// Install target is $project/.agents/skills/ — a sibling of the
527		// plugin root that shares a common ancestor, so filepath.Rel
528		// produces a short relative path.
529		dstDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".agents", "skills")

531		written, err := symlinkSkills(pluginRoot, &InstallMapping{

60	// setupTestEnvironment creates a mock environment for installation testing
61	func setupTestEnvironment(t *testing.T, platform, arch string) *InstallationTestEnvironment {
62		tempDir := t.TempDir()
63		homeDir := filepath.Join(tempDir, "home")
64		err := os.MkdirAll(homeDir, 0755)
65		require.NoError(t, err)
66	
67		env := &InstallationTestEnvironment{
68			Platform:     platform,
69			Architecture: arch,
70			TempDir:      tempDir,
71			HomeDir:      homeDir,
72			ShellType:    "bash", // default
73			t:            t,
74		}
75	
76		// Set environment variables for testing
77		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
78		t.Setenv("DDX_TEST_PLATFORM", platform)
79		t.Setenv("DDX_TEST_ARCH", arch)
80	
81		// Copy the built binary to the expected location for testing
82		// Detect build directory dynamically
83		buildDir := getBuildDir()
84		srcBinary := filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx")
85		if platform == "windows" {
86			srcBinary = filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx.exe")
87		}
88	
89		var destBinary string
90		if platform == "windows" {
91			destBinary = filepath.Join(homeDir, "bin", "ddx.exe")
92			err = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(homeDir, "bin"), 0755)
93			require.NoError(t, err)
94		} else {
95			destBinary = filepath.Join(homeDir, ".local", "bin", "ddx")
96			err = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(homeDir, ".local", "bin"), 0755)
97			require.NoError(t, err)
98		}
99	
100		// Copy the binary if it exists, otherwise create a mock binary for testing
101		if _, err := os.Stat(srcBinary); err == nil {
102			t.Logf("Copying binary from %s to %s", srcBinary, destBinary)
103			srcFile, err := os.Open(srcBinary)
104			require.NoError(t, err)
105			defer func() { _ = srcFile.Close() }()
106	
107			destFile, err := os.Create(destBinary)
108			require.NoError(t, err)
109			defer func() { _ = destFile.Close() }()
110	
111			_, err = io.Copy(destFile, srcFile)
112			require.NoError(t, err)
113	
114			// Make it executable
115			err = os.Chmod(destBinary, 0755)
116			require.NoError(t, err)
117	
118			t.Logf("Binary copied successfully to %s", destBinary)
119		} else {
120			t.Logf("Source binary %s not found: %v, creating mock binary for testing", srcBinary, err)
121			// Create a mock binary for testing
122			mockContent := "#!/bin/bash\necho 'DDx v1.0.0 (test)'\n"
123			if platform == "windows" {
124				mockContent = "@echo off\necho DDx v1.0.0 (test)\n"
125			}
126			err = os.WriteFile(destBinary, []byte(mockContent), 0755)
127			require.NoError(t, err)
128			t.Logf("Mock binary created at %s", destBinary)
129		}
130	
131		return env
132	}

139	// ExecuteInstallCommand simulates executing an installation command
140	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) ExecuteInstallCommand(command string) InstallationResult {
141		start := time.Now()
142	
143		// Determine expected binary path based on platform
144		var expectedBinaryPath string
145		switch env.Platform {
146		case "windows":
147			expectedBinaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, "bin", "ddx.exe")
148		default:
149			expectedBinaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".local", "bin", "ddx")
150		}
151	
152		// For testing, we simulate the installation by copying our built binary
153		buildDir := getBuildDir()
154		builtBinary := filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx")
155		if env.Platform == "windows" {
156			builtBinary = filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx.exe")
157		}
158	
159		// Check if built binary exists
160		if _, err := os.Stat(builtBinary); os.IsNotExist(err) {
161			// For cross-platform testing, if the platform-specific binary doesn't exist,
162			// try to use the current platform's binary for simulation
163			if env.Platform == "windows" && runtime.GOOS != "windows" {
164				// Use Unix binary for Windows simulation in test environment
165				builtBinary = filepath.Join(buildDir, "ddx")
166			}
167	
168			// Check again
169			if _, err := os.Stat(builtBinary); os.IsNotExist(err) {
170				return InstallationResult{
171					Success:                   false,
172					BinaryPath:                "",
173					CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
174					Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Built binary not found at %s", builtBinary),
175					ExitCode:                  1,
176				}
177			}
178		}
179	
180		// Create directory structure
181		installDir := filepath.Dir(expectedBinaryPath)
182		if err := os.MkdirAll(installDir, 0755); err != nil {
183			return InstallationResult{
184				Success:                   false,
185				BinaryPath:                "",
186				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
187				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to create install directory: %v", err),
188				ExitCode:                  1,
189			}
190		}
191	
192		// Copy binary to install location
193		if err := copyFile(builtBinary, expectedBinaryPath); err != nil {
194			return InstallationResult{
195				Success:                   false,
196				BinaryPath:                "",
197				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
198				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to copy binary: %v", err),
199				ExitCode:                  1,
200			}
201		}
202	
203		// Make executable - always make executable for test simulation
204		if err := os.Chmod(expectedBinaryPath, 0755); err != nil {
205			return InstallationResult{
206				Success:                   false,
207				BinaryPath:                "",
208				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
209				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to make binary executable: %v", err),
210				ExitCode:                  1,
211			}
212		}
213	
214		// Configure PATH in shell profile for US-029
215		if err := env.configureShellPath(installDir); err != nil {
216			return InstallationResult{
217				Success:                   false,
218				BinaryPath:                "",
219				CompletedInUnder60Seconds: false,
220				Output:                    fmt.Sprintf("Failed to configure PATH: %v", err),
221				ExitCode:                  1,
222			}
223		}
224	
225		duration := time.Since(start)
226	
227		return InstallationResult{
228			Success:                   true,
229			BinaryPath:                expectedBinaryPath,
230			CompletedInUnder60Seconds: duration < 60*time.Second,
231			Output:                    "Installation completed successfully",
232			ExitCode:                  0,
233		}
234	}

236	// RunCommand simulates running a command in the test environment
237	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) RunCommand(command string) InstallationResult {
238		// Check if DDX binary exists in the expected location
239		var binaryPath string
240		switch env.Platform {
241		case "windows":
242			binaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, "bin", "ddx.exe")
243		default:
244			binaryPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".local", "bin", "ddx")
245		}
246	
247		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx version") {
248			if _, err := os.Stat(binaryPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
249				return InstallationResult{
250					Success:  false,
251					Output:   "ddx: command not found",
252					ExitCode: 127,
253				}
254			}
255	
256			// Execute the version command
257			cmd := exec.Command(binaryPath, "version")
258			output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
259			if err != nil {
260				return InstallationResult{
261					Success:  false,
262					Output:   fmt.Sprintf("Error executing ddx version: %v", err),
263					ExitCode: 1,
264				}
265			}
266	
267			return InstallationResult{
268				Success:  true,
269				Output:   string(output),
270				ExitCode: 0,
271			}
272		}
273	
274		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx doctor") {
275			env.t.Logf("Looking for binary at %s", binaryPath)
276			if _, err := os.Stat(binaryPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
277				env.t.Logf("Binary not found at %s: %v", binaryPath, err)
278				return InstallationResult{
279					Success:  false,
280					Output:   "ddx: command not found",
281					ExitCode: 127,
282				}
283			}
284	
285			// Parse the doctor command and flags
286			args := []string{"doctor"}
287			if strings.Contains(command, "--verbose") {
288				args = append(args, "--verbose")
289			}
290	
291			// Execute the doctor command with flags and timeout
292			ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 30*time.Second)
293			defer cancel()
294			cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, binaryPath, args...)
295			// Set working directory to HomeDir so doctor can find .ddx directory
296			cmd.Dir = env.HomeDir
297			output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
298			if err != nil {
299				return InstallationResult{
300					Success:  false,
301					Output:   fmt.Sprintf("Error executing ddx doctor: %v", err),
302					ExitCode: 1,
303				}
304			}
305	
306			return InstallationResult{
307				Success:  true,
308				Output:   string(output),
309				ExitCode: 0,
310			}
311		}
312	
313		// Handle package manager installation commands
314		packageManagers := []string{"brew install ddx", "sudo apt install ddx", "choco install ddx", "scoop install ddx"}
315		for _, pm := range packageManagers {
316			if strings.Contains(command, pm) {
317				// Simulate package manager installation by executing our install simulation
318				result := env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
319				return result
320			}
321		}
322	
323		// Handle self-update commands
324		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx self-update") {
325			// First install DDX if not already installed
326			if _, err := os.Stat(binaryPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
327				env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
328			}
329	
330			// Create a user config file if it doesn't exist (for preservation test)
331			configPath := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx.yml")
332			if _, err := os.Stat(configPath); os.IsNotExist(err) {
333				configContent := "# DDx configuration\nverbose: false\n"
334				_ = os.WriteFile(configPath, []byte(configContent), 0644)
335			}
336	
337			return InstallationResult{
338				Success:  true,
339				Output:   "Upgrade completed successfully",
340				ExitCode: 0,
341			}
342		}
343	
344		// Handle uninstall commands
345		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx uninstall") {
346			return env.simulateUninstall(command)
347		}
348	
349		// Handle offline install commands
350		if strings.Contains(command, "ddx install --offline") {
351			// Simulate offline installation by executing our install simulation
352			result := env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
353			// Create some library resources to simulate offline package content
354			libPath := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx", "plugins", "ddx")
355			_ = os.MkdirAll(libPath, 0755)
356			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libPath, "README.md"), []byte("# DDx Library"), 0644)
357			return result
358		}
359	
360		return InstallationResult{
361			Success:  false,
362			Output:   fmt.Sprintf("Command not implemented: %s", command),
363			ExitCode: 1,
364		}
365	}

367	// FileExists checks if a file exists in the test environment
368	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) FileExists(path string) bool {
369		// Convert relative paths to absolute paths within test environment
370		if strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {
371			path = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, path[2:])
372		}
373		// Handle Windows environment variables in test context
374		if strings.Contains(path, "%USERPROFILE%") {
375			path = strings.Replace(path, "%USERPROFILE%", env.HomeDir, -1)
376			// Convert Windows path separators to Unix for cross-platform testing
377			path = strings.Replace(path, "\\", "/", -1)
378		}
379		_, err := os.Stat(path)
380		return err == nil
381	}

385		if strings.HasPrefix(path, "~/") {

387		}
388		content, err := os.ReadFile(path)
389		if err != nil {
390			return ""
391		}
392		return string(content)
393	}

956	// configureShellPath configures the shell PATH for the test environment
957	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) configureShellPath(installDir string) error {
958		// Convert absolute path back to tilde notation for the shell config
959		var pathForShell string
960		if strings.HasPrefix(installDir, env.HomeDir) {
961			pathForShell = "~" + installDir[len(env.HomeDir):]
962		} else {
963			pathForShell = installDir
964		}
965	
966		// For Windows testing, use the expected Windows format
967		if env.Platform == "windows" || env.ShellType == "powershell" {
968			pathForShell = "%USERPROFILE%\\bin"
969		}
970	
971		// Determine the shell profile file and path format based on shell type
972		var profileFile, pathEntry string
973	
974		switch env.ShellType {
975		case "bash":
976			profileFile = "~/.bashrc"
977			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("export PATH=\"%s:$PATH\"", pathForShell)
978		case "zsh":
979			profileFile = "~/.zshrc"
980			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("export PATH=\"%s:$PATH\"", pathForShell)
981		case "fish":
982			profileFile = "~/.config/fish/config.fish"
983			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("set -gx PATH %s $PATH", pathForShell)
984		default:
985			profileFile = "~/.profile"
986			pathEntry = fmt.Sprintf("export PATH=\"%s:$PATH\"", pathForShell)
987		}
988	
989		// Resolve profile file path
990		resolvedPath := profileFile
991		if strings.HasPrefix(profileFile, "~/") {
992			resolvedPath = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, profileFile[2:])
993		}
994	
995		// Create profile directory if needed
996		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(resolvedPath), 0755); err != nil {
997			return fmt.Errorf("failed to create profile directory: %w", err)
998		}
999	
1000		// Read existing content
1001		var content string
1002		if existing, err := os.ReadFile(resolvedPath); err == nil {
1003			content = string(existing)
1004		}
1005	
1006		// Check if PATH entry already exists
1007		if strings.Contains(content, installDir) {
1008			return nil // Already configured
1009		}
1010	
1011		// Append PATH configuration
1012		if content != "" && !strings.HasSuffix(content, "\n") {
1013			content += "\n"
1014		}
1015		content += fmt.Sprintf("\n# DDx CLI PATH\n%s\n", pathEntry)
1016	
1017		// Write updated content
1018		return os.WriteFile(resolvedPath, []byte(content), 0644)
1019	}

1021	// setupInstallationState configures the test environment for different installation states
1022	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) setupInstallationState(state string) error {
1023		switch state {
1024		case "healthy":
1025			// Install DDX properly
1026			env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
1027			// Create library directory for doctor check
1028			libDir := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx", "plugins", "ddx")
1029			_ = os.MkdirAll(libDir, 0755)
1030			// Create some sample resources so library appears valid
1031			_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows"), 0755)
1032			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows", "README.md"), []byte("# Workflows"), 0644)
1033			// Create configuration file
1034			configContent := []byte(`version: "1.0"
1035	library:
1036	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
1037	  repository:
1038	    url: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library
1039	    branch: main`)
1040			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx", "config.yaml"), configContent, 0644)
1041			return nil
1042		case "broken_path":
1043			// Install DDX but without PATH configuration
1044			env.ExecuteInstallCommand("install")
1045			// Create library directory for doctor check
1046			libDir := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx", "plugins", "ddx")
1047			_ = os.MkdirAll(libDir, 0755)
1048			// Create some sample resources so library appears valid
1049			_ = os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows"), 0755)
1050			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(libDir, "workflows", "README.md"), []byte("# Workflows"), 0644)
1051			// Create configuration file
1052			configContent := []byte(`version: "1.0"
1053	library:
1054	  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx
1055	  repository:
1056	    url: https://github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx-library
1057	    branch: main`)
1058			_ = os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx", "config.yaml"), configContent, 0644)
1059			// Remove PATH configuration from shell profiles
1060			profileFiles := []string{"~/.bashrc", "~/.zshrc", "~/.profile"}
1061			for _, profileFile := range profileFiles {
1062				if strings.HasPrefix(profileFile, "~/") {
1063					profileFile = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, profileFile[2:])
1064				}
1065				if _, err := os.Stat(profileFile); err == nil {
1066					content, _ := os.ReadFile(profileFile)
1067					// Remove DDX PATH lines
1068					lines := strings.Split(string(content), "\n")
1069					var newLines []string
1070					skipNext := false
1071					for _, line := range lines {
1072						if skipNext && strings.Contains(line, "DDx CLI PATH") {
1073							skipNext = false
1074							continue
1075						}
1076						if strings.Contains(line, "# DDx CLI PATH") {
1077							skipNext = true
1078							continue
1079						}
1080						if strings.Contains(line, ".local/bin") && strings.Contains(line, "PATH") {
1081							continue
1082						}
1083						newLines = append(newLines, line)
1084					}
1085					_ = os.WriteFile(profileFile, []byte(strings.Join(newLines, "\n")), 0644)
1086				}
1087			}
1088			return nil
1089		default:
1090			return fmt.Errorf("unknown installation state: %s", state)
1091		}
1092	}

1094	// simulateUninstall simulates the uninstall process
1095	func (env *InstallationTestEnvironment) simulateUninstall(command string) InstallationResult {
1096		// Remove DDX binary
1097		binaryPaths := []string{
1098			filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".local", "bin", "ddx"),
1099			filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, "bin", "ddx.exe"),
1100		}
1101	
1102		for _, binPath := range binaryPaths {
1103			if _, err := os.Stat(binPath); err == nil {
1104				_ = os.Remove(binPath)
1105			}
1106		}
1107	
1108		// Clean PATH configuration from shell profiles
1109		profileFiles := []string{"~/.bashrc", "~/.zshrc", "~/.profile"}
1110		for _, profileFile := range profileFiles {
1111			if strings.HasPrefix(profileFile, "~/") {
1112				profileFile = filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, profileFile[2:])
1113			}
1114			if _, err := os.Stat(profileFile); err == nil {
1115				content, _ := os.ReadFile(profileFile)
1116				lines := strings.Split(string(content), "\n")
1117				var newLines []string
1118				skipNext := false
1119				for _, line := range lines {
1120					if strings.Contains(line, "# DDx CLI PATH") {
1121						skipNext = true
1122						continue
1123					}
1124					if skipNext && (strings.Contains(line, "export PATH") || strings.Contains(line, "set -gx PATH")) {
1125						skipNext = false
1126						continue
1127					}
1128					newLines = append(newLines, line)
1129				}
1130				_ = os.WriteFile(profileFile, []byte(strings.Join(newLines, "\n")), 0644)
1131			}
1132		}
1133	
1134		// Handle user data based on flags
1135		if strings.Contains(command, "--remove-all") {
1136			// Remove user config
1137			configPath := filepath.Join(env.HomeDir, ".ddx.yml")
1138			_ = os.Remove(configPath)
1139		}
1140		// If --preserve-data is specified or no specific flag, keep user config
1141	
1142		return InstallationResult{
1143			Success:  true,
1144			Output:   "Uninstall completed successfully",
1145			ExitCode: 0,
1146		}
1147	}

296	func mustBuildInstallTarball(t *testing.T, rootName string, manifest string) []byte {
297		t.Helper()
298	
299		var buf bytes.Buffer
300		gz := gzip.NewWriter(&buf)
301		tw := tar.NewWriter(gz)
302	
303		writeDir := func(name string) {
304			t.Helper()
305			if !strings.HasSuffix(name, "/") {
306				name += "/"
307			}
308			require.NoError(t, tw.WriteHeader(&tar.Header{
309				Name:     name,
310				Mode:     0o755,
311				Typeflag: tar.TypeDir,
312			}))
313		}
314		writeFile := func(name, body string) {
315			t.Helper()

version: 1.0.0
description: Sample plugin
type: plugin
source: https://example.com/sample-plugin
api_version: 1
install:

  skills:
    - source: .agents/skills/
      target: .agents/skills/
`), 0o644))
	skillDir := filepath.Join(localPlugin, ".agents", "skills", "sample-skill")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(skillDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(skillDir, "SKILL.md"), []byte(`---
name: sample-skill
description: Sample skill
---

Sample body.
`), 0o644))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
	output, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "install", "sample-plugin", "--local", localPlugin)
	require.NoError(t, err, output)

	for _, sub := range []string{".ddx/plugins/sample-plugin", ".agents/skills", ".claude/skills"} {
		walkRoot := filepath.Join(workDir, filepath.FromSlash(sub))
		require.NoError(t, filepath.Walk(walkRoot, func(path string, info os.FileInfo, walkErr error) error {
			if walkErr != nil {
				if os.IsNotExist(walkErr) {
					return nil
				}
				return walkErr
			}
			if info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0 {
				t.Errorf("FEAT-015: unexpected symlink at %s", path)
			}
			return nil
		}))
	}

	// And no plugin tree leaked into $HOME.
	homePluginDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".ddx", "plugins", "sample-plugin")
	_, statErr := os.Stat(homePluginDir)
	assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr), "FEAT-015: nothing must land under $HOME/.ddx/plugins/")
}

data.text:
Bead complete. Summary of changes:

- **`InstallPackage(pkg, projectRoot)`**: new signature; rejects `~`-rooted `Root.Target` with FEAT-015 error; extracts to `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`; routes skills through `skills.Install`; deleted legacy `~`-symlink and `symlinkSkills`/`pruneStaleSkillLinks` machinery.
- **`installLocal`**: copies plugin into project (no developer-checkout symlinks); rejects `~`-rooted targets; routes skills through `skills.Install`.
- **`--global` removed**: deleted `install_global.go` + test; removed flag from `install.go`.
- **`BuiltinRegistry`**: helix `Root.Target` now project-local; removed `~`-rooted skill mappings.
- **Tests**: `TestInstall_RejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget`, `TestInstall_NoSymlinksCreated`, `TestInstall_GlobalFlagRemoved`, `TestInstallSkills_NoSymlinksAnywhere`, `TestInstallLocalRejectsHomeRootedManifestTarget`; rewrote `TestInstallLocalPreservesExistingProjectPluginDirUnlessForced` for project-local layout; updated `installation_acceptance_test.go` to assert project-local plugin path.
- **Callers updated**: `init.go`, `update.go`, GraphQL resolver pass `workingDir` to `InstallPackage`.
